allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking makes delays visible as part of the larger causal structure so teams stop misreading lagged results as immediate truth.
allies.source_quote: Delays themselves are one of the most powerful leverage points in a system.

allies.rationale_text: Feedback-loops and delays belong together because the timing of the return signal determines whether the system learns or overreacts.
allies.source_quote: Analyzing delayed feedback helps managers understand why their corrective actions might be too aggressive or too timid, leading to oscillation or drift in system performance.

allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory supports delays when paced sequencing gives working memory time to absorb one manageable load at a time.
allies.source_quote: Since working memory is a limited resource, complex topics must be broken down temporally, focusing on steps or stages in a sequence.

antagonists.rationale_text: Naive prioritization can clash with delays when urgency ranking favors short-latency tasks and ignores the high-impact work whose signal arrives later.
antagonists.source_quote: delays conflicts with prioritization (prioritization) when urgency ranking ignores lagged effects and rewards short-latency tasks over high-impact work.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias exploits delays by filling the lag with self-serving interpretations before the true outcome becomes visible.
antagonists.source_quote: delays conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when the lag between action and feedback creates space for confirming interpretations of ambiguous signals before the true outcome becomes visible.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Delays conflicts with prioritization when urgency ranking ignores lagged effects and rewards short-latency tasks over high-impact work.
structured_tensions.source_quote: delays vs prioritization: delays conflicts with prioritization (prioritization) when urgency ranking ignores lagged effects and rewards short-latency tasks over high-impact work.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Delays conflicts with reasoning-mode-router when route decisions are made on incomplete signals before lagged outcomes materialize.
structured_tensions.source_quote: delays vs reasoning-mode-router: delays conflicts with reasoning-mode-router (reasoning mode router) when route decisions are made on incomplete signals before lagged outcomes materialize.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Delays conflicts with confirmation-bias when the lag between action and feedback creates space for confirming interpretations of ambiguous signals before the true outcome becomes visible.
structured_tensions.source_quote: delays vs confirmation-bias: delays conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when the lag between action and feedback creates space for confirming interpretations of ambiguous signals before the true outcome becomes visible.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on delayed-feedback allies and an explicit structured tension block around urgency, routing before signal maturity, and interpretation drift.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor scheduling or productivity overlays beyond the bounded timing, feedback, and explicit tension surface.
open_questions: Leverage-points is source-real as an ally, but systems-thinking, feedback-loops, and cognitive-load-theory were the cleaner bounded anchors for this pass.
